heading: Child health quality measures
label: 1320b–9a Child health quality measures

Effective DateSection and amendment by effective , and applicable to child health assistance and medical assistance provided on or after that date, with certain exceptions, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 111–3section 3 of Pub. L. 111–3section 1396 of this titleApr. 1, 2009

children.heading: Development of an initial core set of health care quality measures for children enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP
children.label: a Development of an initial core set of health care quality measures for children enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall identify and publish for general comment an initial, recommended core set of child health quality measures for use by State programs administered under subchapters XIX and XXI, health insurance issuers and managed care entities that enter into contracts with such programs, and providers of items and services under such programs.January 1, 2010

children.heading: Identification of initial core measures
children.label: 2 Identification of initial core measures
children.text: In consultation with the individuals and entities described in subsection (b)(3), the Secretary shall identify existing quality of care measures for children that are in use under public and privately sponsored health care coverage arrangements, or that are part of reporting systems that measure both the presence and duration of health insurance coverage over time.

children.label: A
children.text: The duration of children’s health insurance coverage over a 12-month time period.

children.label: i
children.text: preventive services, treatments, and services for acute conditions, including services to promote healthy birth, prevent and treat premature birth, and detect the presence or risk of physical or mental conditions that could adversely affect growth and development; and

children.label: ii
children.text: treatments to correct or ameliorate the effects of physical and mental conditions, including chronic conditions and, with respect to dental care, conditions requiring the restoration of teeth, relief of pain and infection, and maintenance of dental health, in infants, young children, school-age children, and adolescents.

children.label: C
children.text: The availability of care in a range of ambulatory and inpatient health care settings in which such care is furnished.

children.label: D
children.text: The types of measures that, taken together, can be used to estimate the overall national quality of health care for children, including children with special needs, and to perform comparative analyses of pediatric health care quality and racial, ethnic, and socioeconomic disparities in child health and health care for children.

children.heading: Encourage voluntary and standardized reporting and mandatory reporting
children.label: 4 Encourage voluntary and standardized reporting and mandatory reporting

children.heading: Voluntary reporting
children.label: A Voluntary reporting
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary, in consultation with States, shall develop a standardized format for reporting information and procedures and approaches that encourage States to use the initial core measurement set to voluntarily report information regarding the quality of pediatric health care under subchapters XIX and XXI.February 4, 2009

children.heading: Mandatory reporting
children.label: B Mandatory reporting
children.text: Beginning with the annual State report on fiscal year 2024 required under subsection (c)(1), the Secretary shall require States to use the initial core measurement set and any updates or changes to that set to report information regarding the quality of pediatric health care under subchapters XIX and XXI using the standardized format for reporting information and procedures developed under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Adoption of best practices in implementing quality programs
children.label: 5 Adoption of best practices in implementing quality programs
children.text: The Secretary shall disseminate information to States regarding best practices among States with respect to measuring and reporting on the quality of health care for children, and shall facilitate the adoption of such best practices. In developing best practices approaches, the Secretary shall give particular attention to State measurement techniques that ensure the timeliness and accuracy of provider reporting, encourage provider reporting compliance, encourage successful quality improvement strategies, and improve efficiency in data collection using health information technology.

children.label: i
children.text: quality related to the duration and stability of health insurance coverage for children under subchapters XIX and XXI;

children.label: ii
children.text: the quality of children’s health care under such subchapters, including preventive health services, dental care, health care for acute conditions, chronic health care, and health services to ameliorate the effects of physical and mental conditions and to aid in growth and development of infants, young children, school-age children, and adolescents with special health care needs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the quality of children’s health care under such subchapters across the domains of quality, including clinical quality, health care safety, family experience with health care, health care in the most integrated setting, and elimination of racial, ethnic, and socioeconomic disparities in health and health care;

children.label: B
children.text: the status of voluntary reporting by States under subchapters XIX and XXI, utilizing the initial core quality measurement set and, beginning with the report required on , and for each annual report thereafter, the status of mandatory reporting by States under subchapters XIX and XXI, utilizing the initial core quality measurement set and any updates or changes to that set; andJanuary 1, 2025

children.label: C
children.text: any recommendations for legislative changes needed to improve the quality of care provided to children under subchapters XIX and XXI, including recommendations for quality reporting by States.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 7 Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide technical assistance to States to assist them in adopting and utilizing core child health quality measures in administering the State plans under subchapters XIX and XXI.

children.label: A
children.text: provide information regarding the quality of health coverage and health care for children;

children.label: B
children.text: address the needs of children throughout the developmental age span; and

children.label: C
children.text: allow purchasers, families, and health care providers to understand the quality of care in relation to the preventive needs of children, treatments aimed at managing and resolving acute conditions, and diagnostic and treatment services whose purpose is to correct or ameliorate physical, mental, or developmental conditions that could, if untreated or poorly treated, become chronic.

children.heading: Advancing and improving pediatric quality measures
children.label: b Advancing and improving pediatric quality measures

children.heading: Establishment of pediatric quality measures program
children.label: 1 Establishment of pediatric quality measures program
A
children.text: improve and strengthen the initial core child health care quality measures established by the Secretary under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: expand on existing pediatric quality measures used by public and private health care purchasers and advance the development of such new and emerging quality measures; and

children.label: C
children.text: increase the portfolio of evidence-based, consensus pediatric quality measures available to public and private purchasers of children’s health care services, providers, and consumers.

children.label: A
children.text: evidence-based and, where appropriate, risk adjusted;

children.label: B
children.text: designed to identify and eliminate racial and ethnic disparities in child health and the provision of health care;

children.label: C
children.text: designed to ensure that the data required for such measures is collected and reported in a standard format that permits comparison of quality and data at a State, plan, and provider level;

children.label: D
children.text: periodically updated; and

children.label: E
children.text: responsive to the child health needs, services, and domains of health care quality described in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of subsection (a)(6)(A).

children.heading: Process for pediatric quality measures program
children.label: 3 Process for pediatric quality measures program
A
children.text: States;

children.label: B
children.text: pediatricians, children’s hospitals, and other primary and specialized pediatric health care professionals (including members of the allied health professions) who specialize in the care and treatment of children, particularly children with special physical, mental, and developmental health care needs;

children.label: C
children.text: dental professionals, including pediatric dental professionals;

children.label: D
children.text: health care providers that furnish primary health care to children and families who live in urban and rural medically underserved communities or who are members of distinct population sub-groups at heightened risk for poor health outcomes;

children.label: E
children.text: national organizations representing children, including children with disabilities and children with chronic conditions;

children.label: F
children.text: national organizations representing consumers and purchasers of children’s health care;

children.label: G
children.text: national organizations and individuals with expertise in pediatric health quality measurement; and

children.label: H
children.text: voluntary consensus standards setting organizations and other organizations involved in the advancement of evidence-based measures of health care.

children.heading: Developing, validating, and testing a portfolio of pediatric quality measures
children.label: 4 Developing, validating, and testing a portfolio of pediatric quality measures
A
children.text: award grants and contracts for the development, testing, and validation of new, emerging, and innovative evidence-based measures for children’s health care services across the domains of quality described in clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of subsection (a)(6)(A); and

children.label: i
children.text: the development of consensus on evidence-based measures for children’s health care services;

children.label: ii
children.text: the dissemination of such measures to public and private purchasers of health care for children; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the updating of such measures as necessary.

children.heading: Revising, strengthening, and improving initial core measures
children.label: 5 Revising, strengthening, and improving initial core measures
children.text: Beginning no later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall publish recommended changes to the core measures described in subsection (a) that shall reflect the testing, validation, and consensus process for the development of pediatric quality measures described in subsection paragraphs (1) through (4).January 1, 20131So in original.1

children.heading: Definition of pediatric quality measure
children.label: 6 Definition of pediatric quality measure
children.text: In this subsection, the term “pediatric quality measure” means a measurement of clinical care that is capable of being examined through the collection and analysis of relevant information, that is developed in order to assess 1 or more aspects of pediatric health care quality in various institutional and ambulatory health care settings, including the structure of the clinical care system, the process of care, the outcome of care, or patient experiences in care.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 7 Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as supporting the restriction of coverage, under subchapter XIX or XXI or otherwise, to only those services that are evidence-based.

children.heading: Annual State reports regarding State-specific quality of care measures applied under Medicaid or CHIP
children.label: c Annual State reports regarding State-specific quality of care measures applied under Medicaid or CHIP
A
children.text: State-specific child health quality measures applied by the States under such plans, including measures described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(6) and, beginning with the annual report on fiscal year 2024, all of the core measures described in subsection (a) and any updates or changes to those measures; and

children.label: B
children.text: State-specific information on the quality of health care furnished to children under such plans, including information collected through external quality reviews of managed care organizations under and benchmark plans under sections 1396u–7 and 1397cc of this title.section 1396u–2 of this title

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 2 Publication
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the Secretary shall collect, analyze, and make publicly available the information reported by States under paragraph (1).September 30, 2010

children.heading: Demonstration projects for improving the quality of children’s health care and the use of health information technology
children.label: d Demonstration projects for improving the quality of children’s health care and the use of health information technology
A
children.text: experiment with, and evaluate the use of, new measures of the quality of children’s health care under such subchapters (including testing the validity and suitability for reporting of such measures);

children.label: B
children.text: promote the use of health information technology in care delivery for children under such subchapters;

children.label: C
children.text: evaluate provider-based models which improve the delivery of children’s health care services under such subchapters, including care management for children with chronic conditions and the use of evidence-based approaches to improve the effectiveness, safety, and efficiency of health care services for children; or

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrate the impact of the model electronic health record format for children developed and disseminated under subsection (f) on improving pediatric health, including the effects of chronic childhood health conditions, and pediatric health care quality as well as reducing health care costs.

children.label: A
children.text: only 1 demonstration project funded under a grant awarded under this subsection shall be conducted in a State; and

children.label: B
children.text: demonstration projects funded under grants awarded under this subsection shall be conducted evenly between States with large urban areas and States with large rural areas.

children.heading: Authority for multistate projects
children.label: 3 Authority for multistate projects
children.text: A demonstration project conducted with a grant awarded under this subsection may be conducted on a multistate basis, as needed.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: $20,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (i) for a fiscal year shall be used to carry out this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: identify, through self-assessment, behavioral risk factors for obesity among children;

children.label: B
children.text: identify, through self-assessment, needed clinical preventive and screening benefits among those children identified as target individuals on the basis of such risk factors;

children.label: C
children.text: provide ongoing support to such target individuals and their families to reduce risk factors and promote the appropriate use of preventive and screening benefits; and

children.label: D
children.text: be designed to improve health outcomes, satisfaction, quality of life, and appropriate use of items and services for which medical assistance is available under subchapter XIX or child health assistance is available under subchapter XXI among such target individuals.

children.heading: Eligibility entities2So in original. Probably should be “Eligible entities”.2
children.label: 2 Eligibility entities2So in original. Probably should be “Eligible entities”.2
A
children.text: A city, county, or Indian tribe.

children.label: B
children.text: A local or tribal educational agency.

children.label: C
children.text: An accredited university, college, or community college.

children.label: D
children.text: A Federally-qualified health center.

children.label: E
children.text: A local health department.

children.label: F
children.text: A health care provider.

children.label: G
children.text: A community-based organization.

children.label: H
children.text: Any other entity determined appropriate by the Secretary, including a consortia or partnership of entities described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (G).3So in original. Probably should be “consortium”.3

children.label: i
children.text: forming partnerships with entities, including schools and other facilities providing recreational services, to establish programs for after school and weekend community activities that are designed to reduce childhood obesity;

children.label: ii
children.text: forming partnerships with daycare facilities to establish programs that promote healthy eating behaviors and physical activity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: developing and evaluating community educational activities targeting good nutrition and promoting healthy eating behaviors;

children.label: I
children.text: after hours physical activity programs; and

children.label: II
children.text: science-based interventions with multiple components to prevent eating disorders including nutritional content, understanding and responding to hunger and satiety, positive body image development, positive self-esteem development, and learning life skills (such as stress management, communication skills, problemsolving and decisionmaking skills), as well as consideration of cultural and developmental issues, and the role of family, school, and community;

children.label: ii
children.text: providing education and training to educational professionals regarding how to promote a healthy lifestyle and a healthy school environment for children;

children.label: iii
children.text: planning and implementing a healthy lifestyle curriculum or program with an emphasis on healthy eating behaviors and physical activity; and

children.label: iv
children.text: planning and implementing healthy lifestyle classes or programs for parents or guardians, with an emphasis on healthy eating behaviors and physical activity for children;

children.label: i
children.text: promoting healthy eating behaviors and physical activity services to treat or prevent eating disorders, being overweight, and obesity;

children.label: ii
children.text: providing patient education and counseling to increase physical activity and promote healthy eating behaviors;

children.label: iii
children.text: training health professionals on how to identify and treat obese and overweight individuals which may include nutrition and physical activity counseling; and

children.label: iv
children.text: providing community education by a health professional on good nutrition and physical activity to develop a better understanding of the relationship between diet, physical activity, and eating disorders, obesity, or being overweight; and

children.label: i
children.text: educate families regarding the relationship between nutrition, eating habits, physical activity, and obesity;

children.label: ii
children.text: educate families about effective strategies to improve nutrition, establish healthy eating patterns, and establish appropriate levels of physical activity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: educate and guide parents regarding the ability to model and communicate positive health behaviors.

children.label: A
children.text: that demonstrate that they have previously applied successfully for funds to carry out activities that seek to promote individual and community health and to prevent the incidence of chronic disease and that can cite published and peer-reviewed research demonstrating that the activities that the entities propose to carry out with funds made available under the grant are effective;

children.label: B
children.text: that will carry out programs or activities that seek to accomplish a goal or goals set by the State in the Healthy People 2010 plan of the State;

children.label: C
children.text: that provide non-Federal contributions, either in cash or in-kind, to the costs of funding activities under the grants;

children.label: D
children.text: that develop comprehensive plans that include a strategy for extending program activities developed under grants in the years following the fiscal years for which they receive grants under this subsection;

children.label: E
children.text: located in communities that are medically underserved, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: F
children.text: located in areas in which the average poverty rate is at least 150 percent or higher of the average poverty rate in the State involved, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: community-based organizations;

children.label: ii
children.text: local governments;

children.label: iii
children.text: local educational agencies;

children.label: iv
children.text: the private sector;

children.label: v
children.text: State or local departments of health;

children.label: vi
children.text: accredited colleges, universities, and community colleges;

children.label: vii
children.text: health care providers;

children.label: viii
children.text: State and local departments of transportation and city planning; and

children.label: ix
children.text: other entities determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Initial design
children.label: A Initial design
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall design the demonstration project. The demonstration should draw upon promising, innovative models and incentives to reduce behavioral risk factors. The Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services shall consult with the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the Director of the Office of Minority Health, the heads of other agencies in the Department of Health and Human Services, and such professional organizations, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, on the design, conduct, and evaluation of the demonstration.February 4, 2009

children.heading: Number and project areas
children.label: B Number and project areas
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall award 1 grant that is specifically designed to determine whether programs similar to programs to be conducted by other grantees under this subsection should be implemented with respect to the general population of children who are eligible for child health assistance under State child health plans under subchapter XXI in order to reduce the incidence of childhood obesity among such population.February 4, 2009

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 6 Report to Congress
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date the Secretary implements the demonstration project under this subsection, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that describes the project, evaluates the effectiveness and cost effectiveness of the project, evaluates the beneficiary satisfaction under the project, and includes any such other information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Federally-qualified health center
children.label: A Federally-qualified health center
children.text: The term “Federally-qualified health center” has the meaning given that term in section 1396d()(2)(B) of this title.l

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: B Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given that term in .section 1603 of title 25

children.label: I
children.text: behavioral risk factors;

children.label: II
children.text: needed preventive and screening services; and

children.label: III
children.text: target individuals’ preferences for receiving follow-up information;

children.label: ii
children.text: is assessed using such computer generated assessment programs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: allows for the provision of such ongoing support to the individual as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: the results of a self-assessment given to the individual;

children.label: II
children.text: behavior modification based on the self-assessment; and

children.label: III
children.text: any need for clinical preventive and screening services or treatment including medical nutrition therapy;

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide any target individual with referrals to community resources and programs available to assist the target individual in reducing health risks; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to provide the information described in clause (i) to a health care provider, if designated by the target individual to receive such information.

children.heading: Appropriation
children.label: 8 Appropriation
children.text: Out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, there is appropriated to carry out this subsection, $25,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2010 through 2014,, $10,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2016 and 2017, and $30,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2018 through 2023.1

children.heading: Development of model electronic health record format for children enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP
children.label: f Development of model electronic health record format for children enrolled in Medicaid or CHIP
A
children.text: subject to State laws, accessible to parents, caregivers, and other consumers for the sole purpose of demonstrating compliance with school or leisure activity requirements, such as appropriate immunizations or physicals;

children.label: B
children.text: designed to allow interoperable exchanges that conform with Federal and State privacy and security requirements;

children.label: C
children.text: structured in a manner that permits parents and caregivers to view and understand the extent to which the care their children receive is clinically appropriate and of high quality; and

children.label: D
children.text: capable of being incorporated into, and otherwise compatible with, other standards developed for electronic health records.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: $5,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (i) for a fiscal year shall be used to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Study of pediatric health and health care quality measures
children.label: g Study of pediatric health and health care quality measures
A
children.text: consider all of the major national population-based reporting systems sponsored by the Federal Government that are currently in place, including reporting requirements under Federal grant programs and national population surveys and estimates conducted directly by the Federal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: identify the information regarding child health and health care quality that each system is designed to capture and generate, the study and reporting periods covered by each system, and the extent to which the information so generated is made widely available through publication;

children.label: C
children.text: identify gaps in knowledge related to children’s health status, health disparities among subgroups of children, the effects of social conditions on children’s health status and use and effectiveness of health care, and the relationship between child health status and family income, family stability and preservation, and children’s school readiness and educational achievement and attainment; and

children.label: D
children.text: make recommendations regarding improving and strengthening the timeliness, quality, and public transparency and accessibility of information about child health and health care quality.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 2 Funding
children.text: Up to $1,000,000 of the amount appropriated under subsection (i) for a fiscal year shall be used to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: h Rule of construction
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision in this section, no evidence based quality measure developed, published, or used as a basis of measurement or reporting under this section may be used to establish an irrebuttable presumption regarding either the medical necessity of care or the maximum permissible coverage for any individual child who is eligible for and receiving medical assistance under subchapter XIX or child health assistance under subchapter XXI.

children.label: A
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2013, $45,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section (other than subsection (e));

children.label: B
children.text: for the period of fiscal years 2016 and 2017, $20,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section (other than subsections (e), (f), and (g));

children.label: C
children.text: for the period of fiscal years 2018 through 2023, $90,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section (other than subsections (e), (f), and (g));

children.label: D
children.text: for the period of fiscal years 2024 through 2027, $60,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section (other than subsections (e), (f), and (g)); and

children.label: E
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2028 and 2029, $15,000,000 for the purpose of carrying out this section (other than subsections (e), (f), and (g)).

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: Funds appropriated under this subsection shall remain available until expended.
